ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To King Childebert.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Childebert, king of the Franks.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The letter of your Excellency has made us exceedingly glad, testifying as it does that you are careful, with pious affection, of the honour and reverence due to priests.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For you thus shew to all that you are faithful worshippers of God, while you love His priests with the acceptable veneration that is due to them, and hasten with Christian devotion to do whatever may advance their position.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Whence also we have received with pleasure what you have written, and grant what you desire with willing mind; and accordingly we have committed, with the favour of God, our vicariate jurisdiction to our brother Virgilius, bishop of the city of Arelate, according to ancient custom and your Excellency’s desire; and have also granted him the use of the pallium, as has been the custom of old.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, inasmuch as some things have been reported to us which greatly offend Almighty God, and confound the honour and reverence due to the priesthood, we beg that they may be in every way amended with the support of the censure of your power, lest, while headstrong and perverse doings run counter to your devotion, your kingdom, or your soul (which God forbid) be burdened by the guilt of others.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, it has come to our knowledge that on the death of bishops some persons from being laymen are tonsured, and mount to the episcopate by a sudden leap.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And thus one who has not been a disciple is in his inconsiderate ambition made a master.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, since he has not learned what to teach, he bears the office of priesthood only in name; for he continues to be a layman in speech and action as before.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: How, then, is he to intercede for the sins of others, not having in the first place bewailed his own?

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For such a shepherd does not defend, but deceives, the flock; since, while he cannot for very shame try to persuade others to do what he does not do himself, what else is it but that the Lord’s people remains a prey to robbers, and catches destruction from the source whence it ought to have had a great support of wholesome protection?

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: How bad and how perverse a proceeding this is let your Excellency’s Highness consider even from your own administration of things.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is certain that you do not put a leader over an army unless his work and his fidelity have first been apparent; unless the virtue and industry of his previous life have shewn him to be a fit person.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if the command of an army is not committed to any but men of this kind, it is easily gathered from this comparison of what sort a leader of souls ought to be.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But it is a reproach to us, and we are ashamed to say it, that priests snatch at leadership who have not seen the very beginning of religious warfare.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But this also, a thing most execrable, has been reported to us as well: that sacred orders are conferred through simoniacal heresy, that is for bribes received.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, seeing that it is exceedingly pestiferous, and contrary to the Universal Church, that one be promoted to any sacred order not for merit but for a price, we exhort your Excellency to order so detestable a wickedness to be banished from your kingdom.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For that man shows himself to be thoroughly unworthy of this office, who fears not to buy the gift of God with money, and presumes to try to get by payment what he deserves not to have through grace.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: These things, then, most excellent son, I admonish you about for this reason, that I desire your soul to be saved.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And I should have written about them before now, had not innumerable occupations stood in the way of my will.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But now that a suitable time for answering your letter has offered itself, I have not omitted what it was my duty to do.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore, greeting your Excellency with the affection of paternal charity, we beg that all things which we have enjoined on our above-named brother and fellow-bishop to be done and observed, may be carried out under the protection of your favour, and that you allow them not to be in any way upset by the elation or pride of any one.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, as they were observed by his predecessor under the reign of your glorious father, so let them be observed now also, by your aid, with zealous devotion.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: It is right, then, that we should thus have a return made to us; and that, as we have not deferred fulfilling your will, so you too, for the sake of God and the blessed Peter, Prince of the apostles, should cause our ordinances to be observed in all respects; that so your Excellency’s reputation, praiseworthy and well-pleasing to God, may extend itself all around.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Given the 12th day of August, Indiction 13.
